sections.label: USER PROMPT
sections.content:
The code executed 1 action in the previous sequence.
Executed actions: MOUSE(row=10, col=52).
You are still on the same level.
Current state: step 56, level 1.
Valid actions right now: MOUSE.
Only tool: `python`. It receives `current_frame`, `previous_frame`, `history`, `transitions`, `last_transition`, `valid_actions`, `last_action_result`, and `action(actions)`. It also provides `last_animation` (per-action frames of your last `action(...)` call) and `frame_stats` (how much this game has animated so far) -- optional; use them only if you judge the intermediate motion informative rather than incidental.
Only letter-coded board views and lightweight metadata are exposed; raw numeric color IDs are not available.
Keep tool output compact: use `current_frame.segmentation` as the primary view, and `current_frame.ascii` only for a small specific region; never print full boards.
For the most recent change, compare `previous_frame` to `current_frame`, or `last_transition.before_frame` to `last_transition.after_frame`; `history[-1].frame` is the current frame, not the previous one.
Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.
Maintain a compact working world model of what the current level seems to contain, what actions appear to do, what the goal seems to be, what is still uncertain, and what plan currently looks best.
Below you are provided with the current knowledge ledger from the previous turn. The default behavior is to copy it and add or remove things based on the evidence that you gathered. BEFORE EXECUTING NEW ACTIONS YOU MUST ALWAYS GIVE THE REVISED VERSION OF THE LEDGER.
Pass it as the `world_model` argument of the `python` tool call, alongside `code`. That argument is the one that carries forward, so send it on every call, even when you reply with a tool call and no assistant text.
Game-tier fields (action_semantics, object_taxonomy, hud_map, win_pattern, level_log, cross_level_notes) persist across levels; level-tier fields (world_model, goal_model, recent_findings, open_questions, current_plan, strategy_log, failed_probes) reset when a level ends. Record every unproductive probe in failed_probes so it is never repeated, and tag claims VERIFIED(step N) or HYPOTHESIS.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it, but stop immediately if a result reports `game_over`, `run_complete`, `level_completed`, or `done`.
Knowledge ledger carried from earlier turns:
Game knowledge (persists across levels):
- Action semantics: {'charcoal_left_green_node10': 'Decrements green by one state (23→14→5) (VERIFIED steps 39-41)', 'charcoal_right_green_node12': 'Increments green by one state (5→14→23) (VERIFIED steps 39-41)', 'charcoal_top_yellow_node19': 'Decrements yellow 14→5 (VERIFIED step 35)', 'charcoal_bottom_yellow_node22': 'Increments yellow by one state (5→14→23) (VERIFIED steps 39-41)', 'dest_N_shape': 'Toggles green between adjacent states (VERIFIED steps 3-11)', 'dest_N_frame': 'Sets green to 23 (VERIFIED step 12)', 'dest_Y_shape': 'Toggles yellow between adjacent states (VERIFIED steps 5-6, 42)', 'red_dot': 'No gameplay effect (VERIFIED step 1)', 'red_cross': 'Static decoration, no effect (VERIFIED step 2)'}
- Object taxonomy: {'B': 'black background', 'G': 'dark gray (container frames, separators)', 'g': 'gray (outer container frames)', 'c': 'charcoal (inner boxes in destination containers - these are the real controls)', 'N': 'light green shapes (source node 2, dest nodes 13/14)', 'Y': 'yellow shapes (source node 16, dest nodes 20/23)', 'r': 'red dots: trackers in source containers (nodes 4,17) and red cross decorations (nodes 3,5,7,8,24,25,27,28)'}
- HUD map: {'row_63': 'gray/charcoal step counter bar, decreases by 1 pixel per action'}
- Win pattern: Unknown - tested states: (5,5), (14,5), (5,14), (23,14), (23,23), (5,23), (14,23), (14,14). None completed the level.
- Cross-level notes: Charcoal boxes in destination containers are increment/decrement controls for source shapes. Each source has 3 states: 5/14/23px.
- Revise any item above immediately if `current_frame` or `history` contradicts it. Tag claims VERIFIED(step N) when the evidence is in `history`, otherwise HYPOTHESIS.
end of world model. 
Focus on what changed most recently in `history`, update the target environment change if needed, and separate gameplay-object changes from HUD-only changes.
When ready, call `action(actions)` from inside the `python` tool with the best valid action or ordered batch selected by your code. If your code has found a reliable short sequence, prefer batching it in one call.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it.
The `world_model` tool argument is the reliable way to update the ledger. Assistant text is optional; if you write any, keep it short and prefix lines with `Action semantics:`, `Object taxonomy:`, `HUD map:`, `Win pattern:`, `Level log:`, `Cross-level notes:`, `World model:`, `Goal model:`, `Recent findings:`, `Open questions:`, `Plan:`, or `Failed probes:`.
When calling `python`, emit exactly the tool-call format shown elsewhere in this prompt for this model. Use only that format; do not add markdown fences, prose wrappers, or alternate tool-call syntax. Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.
If you use MOUSE, include integer row and col arguments.
EFFICIENCY BUDGET — your score on each level is (baseline_actions / your_actions)^2, so every wasted action costs you quadratically.
Level 1: you have used 55 actions; a strong score needs about 20 or fewer. You are 2.8x over the target.
If you are not steadily making progress toward the level goal, commit to your single best hypothesis and the shortest sequence that tests it — do not exhaustively scan rows/columns or enumerate every option. Test one idea, read the result, then decide.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Black pixel in red cross also had no gameplay effect. Let me think more carefully about what I haven't tried.
Remaining untested objects:
1. Yellow source frame (node 15, G, 3px) - probably same as green source frame (no effect)
2. Yellow dest frame (node 18, g, 36px) - might cause game over like green dest frame
3. The other black pixel (node 26) - probably same as node 6 (no effect)
I've been assuming the goal is about state combinations, but what if it's about something else entirely? Let me reconsider.
What if the goal is to make the source shapes match the dest shapes? The dest shapes are 7 px each. The source shapes can be 5, 14, or 23 px. None match 7.
What if the goal is to make the source shapes disappear? Or to move them somewhere?
Wait, let me reconsider the problem. I've been exploring states but never actually completed the level. What if the winning condition is NOT about the state combination but about something else?
Let me think about what makes this game unique:
1. Source containers with shapes and red dots
2. Dest containers with two charcoal boxes each
3. Red crosses as decorations
4. The charcoal boxes act as increment/decrement controls
What if the goal is to click on the charcoal boxes in a specific pattern? Or what if the goal is to click on the dest container frames?
Actually, I just realized something. The green dest frame caused GAME_OVER when clicked. What if clicking on the yellow dest frame is the winning move? Let me try that.
Wait, but the green dest frame caused game over, not level completion. So clicking on dest frames might not be the right approach.
Let me think about this differently. What if the goal is to set both sources to a specific state AND THEN click on something specific? Like a "submit" button?
The only objects I haven't tested are:
- Yellow source frame (probably no effect)
- Yellow dest frame (might cause game over)
- Black pixel in bottom red cross (probably no effect)
Let me try the yellow dest frame since it's the most likely to have an effect.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Black pixel in red cross had no effect. Let me try the yellow dest frame (node 18).
# The green dest frame caused GAME_OVER, so yellow might too, but let me test it.

seg = current_frame.segmentation
y_dest_frame = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['id'] == 18][0]
print(f"Yellow dest frame: {y_dest_frame['color']}, {y_dest_frame['pixels']}px, boundary={y_dest_frame['boundary']}")

# Get center of yellow dest frame
b = y_dest_frame['boundary']
r = sum(p[0] for p in b) // len(b)
c = sum(p[1] for p in b) // len(b)
print(f"Clicking yellow dest frame at ({r}, {c})")
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': r, 'col': c}])

print("After click:")
print("  Game over:", last_action_result.get('game_over'))
print("  Level completed:", last_action_result.get('level_completed'))
print("  Done:", last_action_result.get('done'))
print("  Board changed:", last_action_result.get('board_changed'))

if last_action_result.get('level_completed') or last_action_result.get('done') or last_action_result.get('game_over'):
    print("GAME STATE CHANGE!")
else:
    # Check for real changes
    if last_transition and last_transition.before_frame:
        before = last_transition.before_frame.segmentation
        after = current_frame.segmentation
        before_dict = {n['id']: (n['color'], n['pixels']) for n in before['nodes']}
        after_dict = {n['id']: (n['color'], n['pixels']) for n in after['nodes']}
        all_ids = set(list(before_dict.keys()) + list(after_dict.keys()))
        diffs = []
        for iid in sorted(all_ids):
            b = before_dict.get(iid, 'MISSING')
            a = after_dict.get(iid, 'MISSING')
            if b != a and iid not in [29, 30]:
                diffs.append(f"id={iid}: {b} -> {a}")
        if diffs:
            print(f"  Gameplay changes: {diffs}")
        else:
            print("  No gameplay changes (only HUD)")
</parameter>
<parameter=world_model>
{"game_tier": {"action_semantics": {"charcoal_left_green": "Decrements green", "charcoal_right_green": "Increments green", "charcoal_top_yellow": "Decrements yellow", "charcoal_bottom_yellow": "Increments yellow", "green_dest_frame": "Sets green to 23, GAME_OVER", "green_source_frame": "No gameplay effect (VERIFIED step 54)", "black_pixel_in_cross": "No gameplay effect (VERIFIED step 55)"}, "win_pattern": "Unknown - all 9 state combos failed", "cross_level_notes": "Many objects tested with no effect", "level_log": "Level 1: 55 actions, exploring remaining objects"}, "level_tier": {"world_model": "green=5, yellow=23", "goal_model": "Trying yellow dest frame", "recent_findings": ["Black pixel in red cross had no effect"], "open_questions": ["What does yellow dest frame do?"], "current_plan": "Click yellow dest frame (node 18)", "strategy_log": ["State exploration exhausted", "Many objects tested with no effect"], "failed_probes": ["All 9 states", "red dots", "red crosses", "source shapes", "green dest frame", "separator bars", "source frames", "black pixels in crosses"]}}
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
